clauseList.text: The Supplier may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon five (5) days written notice.
clauseList.simplifiedText: The supplier can end the agreement for any reason with five days notice.
clauseList.explanation: This creates a one-sided termination right that can disrupt operations and negotiating leverage.
clauseList.counterClauseSuggestion: Either party may terminate for convenience upon thirty (30) days written notice, provided all committed deliverables due during that period are completed.

clauseList.text: Invoices are due within fifteen (15) days of receipt.
clauseList.simplifiedText: Invoices must be paid within 15 days.
clauseList.explanation: The payment window is relatively short and may create cash-flow pressure.
clauseList.counterClauseSuggestion: Invoices are due within thirty (30) days of receipt unless otherwise agreed in writing.

clauseList.text: Each party will comply with all applicable privacy laws.
clauseList.simplifiedText: Both parties must follow privacy laws.
clauseList.explanation: This is standard balanced compliance language and does not materially favor one side.

aiOutput.summary: Automated contract review completed with one high-risk clause and one medium-risk commercial term.
recommendations: Negotiate a mutual termination-for-convenience provision with longer notice.
Extend payment timing to net 30 if commercially feasible.

aiOutput.summary: Automated contract review completed with notable termination and payment risks.
recommendations: Update termination rights to be mutual.
Adjust payment timing to reduce short-term cash pressure.

lawyerOutput.reviewNotes: Termination rights should be revised before signature. Payment timing is negotiable but lower priority.
recommendedEdits: Add a 30-day convenience notice period for both parties.
Include a cure period before any non-payment termination trigger.
